original_hypothesis.title: claim() Collects ALL Uniswap Position Fees, Stealing Other Users' Shares and Causing Permanent DoS
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ILOPool.claim() calls pool.collect(type(uint128).max) which sweeps ALL accumulated fees for the entire shared Uniswap V3 position. User's calculated fee share (amount0/amount1) is sent to the token owner, and the remainder (amountCollected - amount) is sent to FEE_TAKER. Since the remainder includes other users' uncollected fees, those fees are permanently stolen. When subsequent users claim, their calculated fee amounts exceed what collect() returns (because earlier collect swept everything), causing underflow at `amountCollected0-amount0` in Solidity 0.7.6 (no overflow checks), wrapping to ~2^256, and reverting the transfer. All subsequent claims are permanently DoS'd.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a fundamental accounting error in how a shared Uniswap V3 position's fees are distributed. Every claim after the first one during a period of fee accumulation will fail, permanently locking user funds.

proof_of_signal.pattern: pool.collect(address(this), TICK_LOWER, TICK_UPPER, type(uint128).max, type(uint128).max) collects ALL owed tokens for shared position; then amountCollected0-amount0 sent to feeTaker where amount0 may include fees not backed by collected tokens for later claimers

preconditions: Pool has been successfully launched (_launchSucceeded == true)
Trading has occurred generating fees on the Uniswap V3 position
Multiple users hold NFT positions with vested liquidity to claim

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: collect(type(uint128).max) sweeps entire position's tokensOwed; fee taker transfer: amountCollected0 - amount0 includes other users' fees
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: First claimer triggers collection of all accumulated fees; fee taker receives other users' portions; subsequent claims revert due to underflow
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: FEE_TAKER receives 100% of non-claiming users' accumulated trading fees as windfall; all other users permanently lose access to both fees and vested liquidity

economic_questions: What is the total fee accumulation rate for typical ILO pool positions?
How many users are typically affected (DoS'd) per pool?
Can a strategic actor time their claim to maximize fee extraction from the shared position?
validation_requirements: Confirm that second claim reverts due to underflow in fee taker transfer
Calculate total value locked per pool that becomes permanently inaccessible
Verify no alternative claim path exists for DoS'd users

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Zero (any position holder can trigger this inadvertently)
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Single claim transaction gas cost (~0.01 ETH)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: FEE_TAKER gains all other users' accumulated fees; DoS'd users lose entire vested position value
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: N/A - primarily a protocol-breaking bug that results in permanent fund loss

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: None - triggered by normal protocol usage
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: After significant fee accumulation on the Uniswap position

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All NFT position holders except the first claimer
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% of remaining vested liquidity + accumulated fees permanently locked
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Entire ILO pool's post-launch value for all but first claimer

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Shared Uniswap V3 position key = keccak256(ILOPool address, TICK_LOWER, TICK_UPPER)
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Trace second claim: burn returns correct amounts, but collect returns only post-first-claim fees; feeGrowthDelta covers full period; underflow confirmed

validation_result.mechanism: shared position fee sweep via collect(max)
validation_result.impact_summary: claim() collects ALL Uniswap V3 position fees (including other users' shares) and sends the excess to FEE_TAKER. Subsequent claimers' transactions revert because their calculated fee entitlements exceed the tokens available from collect(), permanently DoS'ing all remaining claims.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The ILOPool contract manages a single shared Uniswap V3 position for all users. When any user calls claim(), the function: (1) calls pool.burn() which settles ALL accumulated fees for the ENTIRE shared position into tokensOwed, (2) calculates the individual user's proportional fee share, (3) calls pool.collect(type(uint128).max) which sweeps ALL tokensOwed, (4) sends the user their calculated share, and (5) sends the remainder (amountCollected - amount) to FEE_TAKER. The remainder includes other users' uncollected fee shares.

For subsequent claimers: pool.burn() adds only principal (no new fees since last position update), collect() returns only principal, but the ILOPool code still calculates a non-zero fee share (because the user's stored feeGrowthInside is still 0/old value while pool's feeGrowthInsideLast was already updated). This means amount0 = principal*(1-platformFee/BPS) + feeShare*(1-perfFee/BPS) exceeds amountCollected0 = principal. The safeTransfer of amount0 to the user fails because the contract lacks sufficient tokens. Under Solidity 0.7.6 (no checked arithmetic), the fee taker subtraction amountCollected0-amount0 would also underflow to ~2^256.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The vulnerable code is clearly present in ILOPool.sol claim(). The collect(type(uint128).max) call on a shared position is the root cause. The fee accounting assumes collected amounts always cover calculated amounts, which fails when fees have been swept by a prior claimer. Solidity 0.7.6 lacks overflow protection, compounding the issue.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: pool.collect(type(uint128).max) sweeps ALL tokensOwed for the shared Uniswap V3 position, including fees owed to all other position holders. After the first claim sweeps everything, subsequent claims receive only burn principal from collect() but calculate non-zero fee shares from feeGrowthDelta, causing amount0 > amountCollected0. The user transfer reverts due to insufficient balance, and the feeTaker subtraction underflows in Solidity 0.7.6.

The fee calculation uses feeGrowthDelta between the pool's current feeGrowthInside and the user's last stored value. For the second claimer, feeGrowthDelta is non-zero (their stored value is still 0/initial), yielding a positive fee share. But these fee tokens were already swept by the first claimer's collect(max). The calculated amount0 (principal + fees) exceeds what collect() actually returns for this second claim.

calling_sequence: 1. User A calls claim(tokenId_A) - first claim after fees accumulate
2. pool.burn(TICK_LOWER, TICK_UPPER, liquidity2Claim_A) - Uniswap settles ALL accumulated fees for entire shared position into tokensOwed
3. pool.collect(address(this), ..., type(uint128).max, type(uint128).max) - sweeps ALL tokensOwed (principal + ALL fees)
4. A receives amount0 (their proportional share), FEE_TAKER receives amountCollected0-amount0 (includes B's fees)
5. User B calls claim(tokenId_B) - second claim
6. pool.burn() - only adds B's principal to tokensOwed (no new fees since A's claim updated position)
7. pool.collect() - returns only B's burn principal (fees were already swept)
8. B's amount0 = principal*(1-platformFee/BPS) + feeShare*(1-perfFee/BPS) where feeShare > 0
9. safeTransfer(token0, B, amount0) REVERTS - contract balance (= amountCollected0 = principal only) < amount0
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The shared Uniswap V3 position at (ILOPool address, TICK_LOWER, TICK_UPPER) accumulates fees for ALL liquidity. burn() settles ALL accumulated fees into tokensOwed for the entire position, not per-user. collect(max) drains ALL tokensOwed. The ILOPool's virtual sub-position accounting (per-user feeGrowthInside tracking) correctly calculates each user's proportional share, but the physical tokens to back those calculations were removed by the first claimer's collect(). When second claimer's calculated amount0 includes their fee share, but collect() only returned burn principal, the contract lacks tokens to fulfill the transfer.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment configuration affects this vulnerability. The bug is in the claim() function's use of collect(type(uint128).max) on a shared position, which is inherent to the code logic regardless of deployment parameters.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Scenario validated: After pool launch and trading fee accumulation, ANY user calling claim() triggers the bug. No malicious intent required - normal protocol usage causes it. First claimer succeeds but FEE_TAKER receives other users' fee shares. All subsequent claimers are permanently DoS'd. The attack is not even an 'attack' - it's an inevitable consequence of the first claim on any pool with non-zero trading fees.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL: (1) Fee theft: FEE_TAKER receives all non-claiming users' accumulated trading fees on every claim. (2) Permanent DoS: After the first claim, all other users' claim() calls revert because calculated fee amounts exceed available tokens. (3) Locked funds: Users cannot access their vested liquidity or accumulated fees. There is no alternative claim path. The only positions that work are those claimed when fee accumulation is exactly zero (practically impossible for active pools).
recommended_mitigations: Track a contract-level running fee balance and only collect the specific amounts needed for each claim, not type(uint128).max
Alternatively, collect(max) but store the surplus in contract-level accounting variables (fee reserves) that other claimers can draw from
Use a pattern similar to Uniswap's NonfungiblePositionManager which tracks per-position tokensOwed independently

Add a fee accumulator pattern: maintain a global fee-per-liquidity-unit accumulator, and on each claim, calculate the user's share from the accumulator delta, ensuring the collected amount covers only what's needed
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy ILOPool with 2+ investors. 2. Launch the pool. 3. Execute swaps on the Uniswap V3 pool to generate trading fees. 4. Have User A call claim() - verify success and that FEE_TAKER receives excess. 5. Have User B call claim() - verify revert due to insufficient token balance. 6. Verify User B's funds are permanently locked.
uncertainties: The exact revert point depends on relative sizes of platform fee vs accumulated trading fees - either the user transfer or the fee taker transfer reverts first

If platformFee is very high and trading fees are very low relative to principal, the second claim might succeed but with incorrect fee accounting (fee taker gets platform fee minus user's fee share instead of correct amount)
additional_testing_needed: Verify exact revert behavior with varying platformFee and performanceFee values
Test with multiple sequential claims to confirm complete DoS after first claim
Quantify the fee theft amount received by FEE_TAKER under various trading volume scenarios
Test edge case where liquidity2Claim = 0 (no vested liquidity) but fees exist
